title: Compose Development, Overrides, Profiles, Watch, and Test Workflows · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Compose Profiles Targeting
summary: Developers need fast source sync and optional debugging while CI needs isolated dependencies, deterministic tests, and no production-only side effects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose profiles targeting increment in development and test Compose workflow matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies.
Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.

  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example override bind-mounts production paths, watch rebuilds on generated files, and dev and test profiles silently change dependencies. Render every file combination and isolate disposable state. Competency probe: Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Profiles Targeting, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to development and test Compose workflow matrix.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-compose-development-testing-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-compose-development-testing-3
    artifact: compose.dev.yaml
    decision: worked docker compose development testing 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      develop:
      watch:
      - action: sync
      path: ./src
      profiles: [dev]
      test:
      profiles: [test]
    verify: docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.dev.yaml config --profiles
    failure: Reject production bind mounts, hidden override precedence, watch-triggered dependency churn, or a profile that changes the core dependency contract.
    repair: Render every file combination, scope development-only state, select sync versus rebuild deliberately, and prove test teardown is isolated.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-compose-profiles-targeting-63qcm
  - competency: docker-compose-profiles-targeting
    artifact: compose.dev.yaml
    decision: compose profiles targeting
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: move a tag while the immutable digest remains available
    retained-proof: timestamped lifecycle transition and bounded cleanup result
    case-key: 63qcm
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      develop:
      watch:
      - action: sync
      path: ./src
      profiles: [dev]
      test:
      profiles: [test]
    verify: docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.dev.yaml config --profiles
    failure: Reject production bind mounts, hidden override precedence, watch-triggered dependency churn, or a profile that changes the core dependency contract.
    repair: Render every file combination, scope development-only state, select sync versus rebuild deliberately, and prove test teardown is isolated.
